refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the Vehicle to complete a load→travel→dump cycle with a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that directly impacts customer productivity and system acceptance. Refinement clarifies that cycle time is a measurable outcome dependent on vehicle performance, operational conditions, and safety constraints. The p95 metric and duty cycle context are essential to avoid over-specification or under-specification of performance.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Instrumented field trials under representative duty cycle conditions, with telemetry capture of cycle phase timestamps (load start, travel start, dump start, dump end). Statistical analysis of ≥100 cycles to establish p95 confidence interval and compare against CT_target.
sub_frs: FR-2.1: The Vehicle shall complete the load phase within a target time envelope.
FR-2.2: The Vehicle shall complete the travel phase (loaded and unloaded) within a target time envelope.
FR-2.3: The Vehicle shall complete the dump phase within a target time envelope.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall monitor and report cycle time telemetry to enable performance tracking and diagnosis.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Optimize vehicle drivetrain power delivery, hydraulic response time, and load handling mechanisms to minimize phase durations; establish baseline cycle time through design simulation and prototype testing.
DP-B: Implement real-time cycle time monitoring via onboard telemetry (phase timestamps, cycle duration), with predictive analytics to identify bottlenecks and enable operator coaching.
DP-C: Integrate tele-operation capability to allow remote operator intervention during cycle phases, reducing operator reaction time and enabling expert-level cycle optimization.

DP-D: Establish a closed-loop performance management system combining vehicle instrumentation, telemetry analysis, safety-gated optimization, and operator feedback to iteratively reduce cycle time within safety constraints.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Establish a closed-loop performance management system combining vehicle instrumentation, telemetry analysis, safety-gated optimization, and operator feedback to iteratively reduce cycle time within safety constraints.

potential_sources: DP-D couples vehicle hardware performance (DP-A) with monitoring/analytics (DP-B) and tele-operation capability (DP-C), but this is intentional and necessary: cycle time is a system-level outcome, not a single-component problem.
Safety criticality (high) introduces a constraint: any optimization must be validated against safety policies before deployment. This is a design constraint, not a coupling defect.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D is a system-level design pattern (closed-loop performance management) that orchestrates multiple subsystems. It avoids the trap of optimizing a single component in isolation. The coupling is managed through explicit safety gates and telemetry-driven feedback.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle hardware (drivetrain, hydraulics, load handling) is capable of achieving CT_target under nominal conditions. Risk: If hardware is fundamentally undersized, no operational optimization will meet the target. Mitigation: Validate hardware capability early via simulation and prototype testing.

Assumption: Telemetry infrastructure (sensors, network, data pipeline) can reliably capture cycle phase timestamps with sufficient fidelity (±100 ms or better). Risk: Noisy or delayed telemetry will corrupt performance analysis. Mitigation: Sensor calibration and network latency testing during integration.

Assumption: Operator and tele-operator behavior can be coached/optimized without violating safety policies. Risk: Safety constraints may be incompatible with aggressive cycle time targets. Mitigation: Early safety-performance trade-off analysis with Safety Manager.

Assumption: Site conditions (soil, slope, load variability) remain within the agreed duty cycle envelope. Risk: Outlier conditions may invalidate the p95 target. Mitigation: Define duty cycle bounds explicitly and monitor for excursions.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-D is sound but should be decomposed into sub-DPs at the next level to clarify the roles of vehicle hardware optimization, telemetry/analytics, tele-operation, and safety governance. This will reduce ambiguity and enable parallel work-stream planning.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary encompassing all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and their orchestration. At this level, the IM assignment reflects that cycle time performance is a system-level outcome requiring coordination across vehicle hardware, telemetry, remote operation, and safety governance.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Cycle phase timestamps, cycle duration, vehicle state (speed, load, hydraulic pressure), operator actions, and performance metrics are transmitted from Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems to external monitoring/analytics systems (Site Manager dashboard, performance database).

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Duty cycle definition, CT_target, site conditions, and operational policies are received from Site Manager and integrated into the NGHE Solution's performance management logic.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety constraints and alerts are exchanged between Safety Services and the Vehicle/Teleoperation subsystems to ensure that cycle time optimization does not violate safety policies.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication backbone for telemetry, tasking, and safety alerts between Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and external systems (Site Manager, Tele-operator, analytics platform).

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level boundary and is the natural home for the closed-loop performance management system. All sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained within NGHE Solution. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Telemetry out, Tasking in, SafetyAlert inout, Network Link inout) cross the boundary.

Vehicle contains the hardware (drivetrain, hydraulics, load handling) and onboard instrumentation for cycle time measurement. However, cycle time optimization logic, analytics, and tele-operation coordination reside outside Vehicle, requiring multiple external interfaces. Vehicle is a critical component but not the sole owner of cycle time performance.

Teleoperation enables remote operator intervention and can reduce operator reaction time, but does not own vehicle hardware performance or safety governance. Cycle time optimization via tele-operation is a partial solution and requires coordination with Vehicle and Safety Services.

Safety Services enforces constraints on cycle time optimization but does not own the performance management system itself. Safety is a cross-cutting concern that must be integrated into the closed-loop system but is not the primary owner of cycle time KPI.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The closed-loop performance management system (DP-D) is best owned by the NGHE Solution as a system-level orchestration layer. This layer coordinates vehicle hardware performance, telemetry collection/analysis, tele-operation capability, and safety governance. Sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are responsible for their respective domains but report performance data and safety status to the NGHE Solution orchestrator.
containment_risks: Risk: If the NGHE Solution orchestration layer is not explicitly designed and owned, responsibility for cycle time performance may diffuse across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, leading to finger-pointing and missed optimization opportunities.

Risk: Telemetry and analytics infrastructure must be robust and low-latency to support real-time feedback. If telemetry is unreliable or delayed, the closed-loop system will fail to optimize effectively.

Risk: Safety constraints may conflict with aggressive cycle time targets. If Safety Services and performance optimization are not tightly integrated, the system may oscillate between safety and performance goals.
Risk: Tele-operator workload and decision-making speed may become a bottleneck if tele-operation is relied upon for cycle time optimization. This requires careful human factors analysis.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Explicitly define the NGHE Solution orchestration layer as a system-level module with clear ownership, interfaces, and responsibilities. Decompose the closed-loop performance management system into sub-modules (telemetry pipeline, analytics engine, optimization logic, safety gate) at the next decomposition level. Establish clear handoff points between Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and the orchestration layer.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR-refined (cycle time ≤ CT_target under duty cycle and safety constraints) is directly addressed by DP-D (closed-loop performance management system). The DP is solution-neutral (does not prescribe specific hardware or algorithms) and is verifiable through field trials. The mapping is 1:1 and non-redundant.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: If the NGHE Solution orchestration layer is not explicitly designed, the closed-loop logic may be scattered across Vehicle firmware, Teleoperation software, and Safety Services, making it difficult to maintain and evolve.

Diffusion Risk 2: Telemetry interfaces (Telemetry out, Network Link inout) must be standardized and implemented consistently across Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems. Inconsistent telemetry formats will break the analytics pipeline.

Diffusion Risk 3: Safety gate logic (SafetyAlert inout) must be centralized and version-controlled. If safety constraints are embedded in multiple subsystems, they may become inconsistent or outdated.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for the closed-loop performance management system. All four known interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are used, indicating good alignment with the system architecture. No new interfaces are required.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-refined is feasible via instrumented field trials. Telemetry interface (Telemetry out) enables capture of cycle phase timestamps and cycle duration. Statistical analysis of p95 cycle time is straightforward. Verification does not require new instrumentation beyond what is already planned for the NGHE Solution.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of cycle time performance to the NGHE Solution is clear. Sub-allocations to Vehicle (hardware performance), Teleoperation (operator intervention), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement) will be defined at the next decomposition level. No ambiguity in ownership or responsibility.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1: Explicitly define the NGHE Solution orchestration layer (system-level module) with clear ownership, interfaces, and responsibilities. This will reduce diffusion risk and clarify the closed-loop performance management system.

Revision 2: Establish a safety-performance trade-off analysis with the Safety Manager early in the design process. Clarify which cycle time optimizations are compatible with safety policies and which are not.
Revision 3: Define telemetry data formats, sampling rates, and latency requirements for the analytics pipeline. Ensure that Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems implement telemetry consistently.
Revision 4: Plan for iterative performance optimization cycles (design → prototype → test → analyze → refine). Establish a feedback loop between field data and design improvements.

decision.justification: FR-refined is well-defined and DP-D is a sound system-level design pattern. However, DP-D is not atomic and requires decomposition into sub-DPs to clarify the roles of vehicle hardware optimization, telemetry/analytics, tele-operation, and safety governance. The NGHE Solution IM boundary is appropriate, but the orchestration layer must be explicitly designed at the next level. Proceeding to the next decomposition level is necessary to reduce ambiguity and enable detailed design and implementation planning.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-2.1: The Vehicle shall complete the load phase within a target time envelope (e.g., ≤ T_load_target).
FR-2.2: The Vehicle shall complete the travel phase (loaded and unloaded) within a target time envelope (e.g., ≤ T_travel_target).
FR-2.3: The Vehicle shall complete the dump phase within a target time envelope (e.g., ≤ T_dump_target).
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect, transmit, and analyze cycle time telemetry in real-time to enable performance tracking, bottleneck identification, and operator coaching.
FR-2.5: The NGHE solution shall enforce safety constraints on cycle time optimization, ensuring that no optimization violates worksite safety policies or operational limits.
FR-2.6: The Tele-operator shall be able to intervene in cycle phases to optimize performance or respond to anomalies, with latency ≤ T_tele_latency_max.

assumptions: Vehicle hardware (drivetrain, hydraulics, load handling) is capable of achieving CT_target under nominal conditions.
Telemetry infrastructure can reliably capture cycle phase timestamps with fidelity ±100 ms or better.
Operator and tele-operator behavior can be coached/optimized without violating safety policies.
Site conditions remain within the agreed duty cycle envelope.
CT_target is achievable and has been validated against customer requirements and site conditions.
Safety policies and operational limits are well-defined and can be formalized as constraints in the optimization logic.
open_risks: Hardware capability risk: If vehicle hardware is fundamentally undersized, no operational optimization will meet CT_target.
Telemetry reliability risk: Noisy or delayed telemetry will corrupt performance analysis and feedback.
Safety-performance conflict risk: Safety constraints may be incompatible with aggressive cycle time targets.
Tele-operator workload risk: Tele-operator decision-making speed may become a bottleneck.
Site condition variability risk: Outlier conditions may invalidate the p95 target.
Orchestration diffusion risk: If the NGHE Solution orchestration layer is not explicitly designed, responsibility for cycle time performance may diffuse across subsystems.
evidence_needed: Vehicle hardware simulation and prototype testing to validate capability against CT_target.
Telemetry sensor specifications and network latency analysis to confirm fidelity and reliability.
Safety-performance trade-off analysis with Safety Manager to clarify compatible optimizations.
Tele-operator workload and decision-making analysis (human factors study) to assess feasibility of remote intervention.
Duty cycle definition and site condition characterization to establish p95 confidence bounds.
NGHE Solution orchestration layer design document (architecture, interfaces, responsibilities) to clarify the closed-loop performance management system.
